question_text: Steam at 100°C condenses into liquid water at 100°C. Why does this process release heat energy?
correct_answer: When gas condenses to liquid, the particles lose kinetic energy and move closer together as they form stronger bonds. This energy is released as heat to the surroundings.
explanation: Condensation is the opposite of evaporation. As gas particles slow down and form a liquid, they release the energy that was used to separate them during evaporation. This energy is transferred to the surroundings as heat.
